content.problemStatement:
We call a non-negative number xxx good if its binary representation is palindromic. For example, 107107107 is good because its binary representation is 110101111010111101011 which is palindromic while 101010 is not good because its binary representation is 101010101010 which is not palindromic.

Recall that, a string is said to be palindromic if it reads the same backward as forward.

You are given a number nnn. You have to express nnn as a sum of at most 121212 good numbers. If there exist multiple answers, print any one of them.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each testcase contains a single integer nnn.


Output Format
For each testcase, print a single integer mmm (1≤m≤121 \le m \le 121≤m≤12), denoting the number of good numbers. In the next line, print a sequence of mmm good numbers whose sum is equal to nnn. If there are multiple such sequences, print any of them.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

212121 is good because 21=(10101)221 = (10101)_221=(10101)2​ which is palindromic.
333 is good because 3=(11)23 = (11)_23=(11)2​ which is palindromic.

Test case 222:

111 is good because 1=(1)21 = (1)_21=(1)2​ which is palindromic.

Here ()2()_2()2​ denotes binary representation of the number.
